subject: Re: warp needs to be restarted to read new movies
body:
I am in the same situation, but have no solution.

This is probably because the FileSystemWatcher, which WARP is using to 
detect file update events, is NOT reliable for network drives...
- 
https://stackoverflow.com/questions/7818545/filesystemwatcher-stops-monitoring-network-folder
- 
https://stackoverflow.com/questions/151804/system-io-filesystemwatcher-to-monitor-a-network-server-folder-performance-con
- 
https://www.codeproject.com/Questions/5298360/Filesystemwatcher-not-triggering-events-windows-10


2021年2月25日木曜日 16:40:11 UTC+9 zly37...@gmail.com:

> Hello,
>
> I am using warp 1.0.9 on-fly to process the movies from our Titan Krios. 
> But usually warp does not recognize the new generated movies and we need to 
> restart it every time to have all the images processed. Warp should refresh 
> and read the new movies just took from the microscope automatically. In our 
> case, this does not work. Any suggestion about how can I fix this issue? 
> TIA!
>
>
>
> Best regards,
> Lingyun
>
